it: Le piastre del condensatore alimentate con la tensione V.
en: The capacitor plates powered with the voltage V.

it: La fig. 1 mostra un'ampolla di vetro
en: Fig. {0} shows a glass bulb in which a vacuum has been made. Inside the bulb two electrodes A and B are arranged; electrode A can be heated by the Joule effect. If a voltage is applied between points A and B (fig. {1}), a flow of current is observed, which can be detected with an ammeter connected in series with the circuit. If electrode A is heated, the current is seen to increase appreciably.

it: Come è possibile che ci sia conduzione di corrente tra due punti separati nel
en: How can there be conduction of current between two separated points in a vacuum?

it: Il fenomeno si può interpretare pensando che esistano delle particelle cariche
en: The phenomenon can be interpreted by imagining that there exist charged particles that can move through the space between the two electrodes. When a potential difference is applied, electrode A emits these charged particles which, moving to the other electrode, form the current in the vacuum. When electrode A is heated, it releases a greater number of particles and therefore allows a greater flow of current.

it: Il dispositivo rappresentato in figura 3 è un ingegnoso sistema
en: The device shown in figure {0} is an ingenious system that allows us to produce a focused beam of negatively charged particles.

it: Consideriamo una particella carica negativa emessa dal filamento caldo A. Nel percorso
en: Consider a negatively charged particle emitted by the hot filament A. Along the path A→B the particle is slowed by a potential difference of about 10 V; this stage serves to regulate the flow of particles: the higher the voltage, the fewer particles pass to the next stage. Along the path B→C the particles are accelerated considerably by a potential difference of the order of 5000 V; this stage serves to give energy to the particles of the beam. Along the paths C→D and D→E the particles are first slowed and then accelerated by a potential difference of the order of 100 V. These two stages constitute two electrostatic lenses, the first converging and the second diverging, which allow the beam to be focused at a certain fixed distance.

it: In uscita da questo sistema si ottiene un fascio di particelle cariche negative veloci
en: At the output of this system one obtains a beam of fast, well-focused negatively charged particles.

it: È chiaro che un cannone di particelle cariche può essere realizzato in molti modi
en: It is clear that a gun of charged particles can be made in many different ways, and that it can have an accelerating voltage much higher or much lower than 5000 V; however, the simple scheme we have shown is sufficient for the experiments we will describe in this card.

it: È interessante provare ad alimentare il sistema con tensioni di segno opposto
en: It is interesting to try powering the system with voltages of the opposite sign. In principle we should obtain a gun of positively charged particles, but one observes that no particle is projected. This can be explained by supposing that a heated filament emits only negatively charged particles.

it: Il fascio di particelle cariche non è direttamente visibile
en: The beam of charged particles is not directly visible. To detect the beam a phosphor screen is used (fig. {0}).

it: Lo schermo ai fosfori è sostanzialmente una lastra di vetro
en: The phosphor screen is essentially a glass plate on which a particular chemical substance is deposited that, when struck by the accelerated charged particles, emits visible light, making the point of incidence luminous.

it: Per ora non ci preoccuperemo di spiegare questo fenomeno di fosforescenza
en: For now we will not concern ourselves with explaining this phenomenon of phosphorescence, but will use it only as an empirical fact that allows us to carry out our experiments.

it: L'apparato di figura 5 è costituito da un cannone elettronico
en: The apparatus in figure {0} consists of an electron gun, a pair of plates that allow us to produce a uniform electric field, and a phosphor screen. Everything is enclosed in a glass chamber in which a vacuum has been made. If the plates are powered with a potential difference, a deflection due to the electric field is observed, exactly as we expected on the assumption that the beam was formed by a set of negatively charged particles. So this experiment provides a confirmation of the hypothesis that a heated conducting filament emits negatively charged particles.

it: Nel sistema di figura 6 le due piastre conduttrici sono state sostituite
en: In the system of figure {0} the two conducting plates have been replaced by two coils. By passing a current through these coils, a magnetic field is generated that causes a deflection of the beam. In this case too the deflection is exactly as we expected on the hypothesis that the beam is composed of a set of charged particles.

it: Ora calcoliamo gli angoli di deflessione
en: Now let us compute the deflection angles {0} and {1} relative to the electric field and to the magnetic field, on the assumption that Newton's laws are valid.

it: Deflessione dovuta al campo elettrico
en: Deflection due to the electric field

it: Deflessione dovuta al campo magnetico
en: Deflection due to the magnetic field

it: Come si vede dalle formule, gli angoli di deflessione dipendono direttamente dal rapporto
en: As can be seen from the formulas, the deflection angles depend directly on the ratio {0} between the charge and the mass of the charged particles. If the beam were composed of different types of particle, with different ratios {1}, then we would have obtained different deflection angles for the different particles, and we would have observed more than one bright spot on the phosphor screen. The fact that we obtain a single deflection angle is proof that the particles emitted by the incandescent filament all have the same charge-to-mass ratio. It is very reasonable to think that they are in fact a single type of particle, with a definite mass and a definite charge. This hypothesis is confirmed by many other experiences, and the particles in question are called electrons.

it: Il dispositivo per l'esperimento di Thomson contiene entrambi i sistemi
en: The device for Thomson's experiment contains both deflection systems: the plates for the electric field and the coils for the magnetic field.

it: Lo scopo dell'esperimento è di determinare il rapporto
en: The aim of the experiment is to determine the ratio {0} between the charge and the mass of the electron, and it is carried out in two steps.

it: Prima si alimentano i due sistemi in opposizione
en: First the two systems are powered in opposition, adjusting the intensities of the electric and magnetic fields so as to observe zero deflection. In this way there is a balance between the magnetic force and the electric force and, from the corresponding equilibrium relation, the speed of the electrons can be obtained:

it: Poi si misura la deflessione dovuta ad uno solo dei due sistemi
en: Then the deflection due to only one of the two systems is measured and, knowing the speed {0}, the ratio {1} is determined. For example, by measuring {2}:

it: Con questi esperimenti abbiamo individuato un'importante particella con carica negativa
en: With these experiments we have identified an important particle with negative charge, the electron. However, we have not yet observed any positive particle: this is because a heated filament emits only electrons, and so the electron gun cannot fire positively charged particles. But we know that matter is essentially neutral, so for every negative charge there must be a corresponding positive charge. In the next experiment we will show that a neutral atom is made up of a certain number of electrons and a positive particle. The positive particles obtained by removing one or more electrons from a neutral atom are called positive ions.

it: La figura 7 mostra l'apparato sperimentale. Abbiamo un tubo di vetro
en: Figure {0} shows the experimental apparatus. We have a glass tube and, at the right and left ends, two phosphor screens. In the central part two perforated electrodes are arranged. Inside the tube there is a certain gas at low pressure (about {1} atmospheres).

it: Se alimentiamo i due elettrodi con una tensione di circa 1000
en: If we power the two electrodes with a voltage of about 1000 V, two bright spots appear on the two screens (fig. {0}): on the right we obtain a beam of negative particles, on the left a beam of positive particles.

it: Possiamo inserire dei sistemi di deflessione per studiare il tipo di particelle
en: We can insert deflection systems to study the kind of particles that are projected, measuring their charge-to-mass ratio {0} (fig. {1}).

it: Eseguendo queste misure si osserva che le particelle negative non dipendono
en: Carrying out these measurements, one observes that the negative particles do not depend on the type of gas introduced into the apparatus, and are the same ones we found with the electron gun: so they are electrons. The positive particles, instead, depend on the type of gas. One also observes that the positive particles undergo a much smaller deflection than that undergone by the electrons under the same electric field. This means that the ratio {0} of the positive particles is much smaller than that of the electrons.

it: In alcuni casi, come mostra la figura 10, le particelle positive generano
en: In some cases, as figure {0} shows, the positive particles produce two or even more bright spots. The additional spots correspond to a ratio {1} that is a multiple of the main one: {2}, {3}, etc.

it: Questo esperimento può essere interpretato pensando che gli atomi siano formati
en: This experiment can be interpreted by imagining that atoms are formed by a positive particle, which we will call an ion, and a certain number of electrons. Most atoms are joined together and form a neutral structure. In some cases, however, there can also be split atoms and, when a voltage is applied to the electrodes, the ion and the electrons are accelerated in opposite directions. In this way, beyond the holes made in the electrodes, beams of charged particles are generated.

it: Le particelle positive subiscono una deflessione molto inferiore a quella degli
en: The positive particles undergo a much smaller deflection than the electrons; therefore, since they must have the same charge {0}, this means that they have a much larger mass {1}. The ratio between the electron mass and the ion mass depends on the atom considered and is of the order of {2}.

it: Il fatto che gli ioni possano generare anche più di un punto luminoso
en: The fact that ions can produce more than one bright spot is explained by the fact that an ion can also be obtained by removing more than one electron from a neutral atom. In this way the charge of the ion becomes a multiple of that of the electron, while the mass remains practically unchanged (because the electron is very light compared with the neutral atom, so removing one electron does not change the mass much); the result is a ratio {0} that is a multiple of the fundamental one and a multiple deflection.

it: A questo punto può sorgere una domanda: quanti elettroni si possono togliere
en: At this point a question may arise: how many electrons can be removed from a neutral atom?

it: Alcuni esperimenti, che non discuteremo in questa scheda, mostrano che gli atomi
en: Some experiments, which we will not discuss in this card, show that atoms are made up of a finite number of electrons. This number depends on the type of atom; it can be just one, as in the case of hydrogen, and can exceed one hundred. The positive particle that remains when all its electrons are removed from an atom is called the nucleus.

it: Fino ad ora abbiamo misurato i rapporti
en: So far we have measured the ratios {0} of the electrons and of the ions, but we have not yet measured the charge {1} and the mass {2} separately. To conclude this card we describe a very ingenious and elegant experiment that allows us to measure the charge {3} of the electron.

it: Le figure 11 e 12 mostrano due foto dell'apparecchiatura.
en: Figures {0} and {1} show two photographs of the apparatus.

it: Il primo elemento da osservare è il nebulizzatore per l'olio ad effetto Venturi
en: The first element to observe is the Venturi-effect oil atomiser, shown schematically in figure {0}. The droplets sprayed by the atomiser enter through two small holes between the conducting plates of a capacitor enclosed under a transparent plastic chamber; figure {1} shows a diagram of the atomiser, the capacitor and the droplets.

it: La figura 15 mostra una foto del condensatore e della camera che lo racchiude.
en: Figure {0} shows a photograph of the capacitor and the chamber enclosing it. The droplets sprayed inside the capacitor are visible through a microscope, shown on the left in figure {1}; the illumination of the inside of the capacitor is provided by a lamp shown on the right in the same figure {2}. The photograph in figure {3} shows what one sees looking through the microscope.

it: Osservando le goccioline si vede che queste cadono sotto l'azione del campo gravitazionale
en: Observing the droplets, one sees that they fall under the action of the gravitational field (actually they are seen to rise because the microscope inverts the image).

it: Se si alimentano le piastre con una certa tensione
en: If the plates are powered with a certain voltage {0} (fig. {1}), one observes that the motion of some droplets changes: some fall faster, some fall more slowly, some others begin to rise. This means that some droplets carry a non-zero electric charge, and therefore experience a force under the action of the electric field.

it: Lo scopo dell'esperimento è di misurare la carica di una delle goccioline
en: The aim of the experiment is to measure the charge of one of the droplets. By adjusting the voltage {0} one can make the chosen droplet stay still in equilibrium. In this condition we have a balance among the various forces: the weight, the Archimedean buoyancy due to the air, and the electric force:

it: In questa equazione g è l'accelerazione di gravità
en: In this equation {0} is the acceleration of gravity, {1} and {2} are the densities of the oil and of the air, {3} is the radius of the droplet and {4} is the electric field present between the capacitor plates. These quantities are all known, except the radius {5} of the droplet; therefore, to derive the charge {6}, we must find a way to measure this radius. The method devised by Millikan is very clever.

it: Si spegne il campo elettrico e la gocciolina inizia a cadere
en: The electric field is switched off and the droplet begins to fall. The falling speed of the droplet increases until it reaches a limiting speed at which the forces due to the weight, the Archimedean buoyancy and the viscous friction with the air balance:

it: In questa equazione abbiamo usato la formula di Stokes per la forza viscosa
en: In this equation we have used Stokes' formula for the viscous force, {0}, where {1} is the viscosity of the air and {2} is the speed of the droplet.

it: Dalla seconda equazione possiamo ricavare il raggio
en: From the second equation we can obtain the radius {0} of the droplet:

it: Ora possiamo ricavare la carica
en: Now we can obtain the charge {0} from the first equation:

it: sostituendo la formula trovata per
en: substituting the formula found for {0}:

it: La formula definitiva per la carica
en: The final formula for the charge {0} is

it: Le grandezze da misurare nell'esecuzione dell'esperimento
en: The quantities to be measured in performing the experiment are the electric field {0} and the falling speed of the droplet {1}. The field {2} is given by the ratio {3} between the applied voltage {4} and the distance {5} between the capacitor plates. The speed {6} is obtained from the ratio {7} between the distance {8} and the time {9}. The distance {10} is measured with a graduated scale visible inside the eyepiece; the time {11} is measured with a manual stopwatch.

it: Le costanti da utilizzare nella formula per la carica
en: The constants to be used in the formula for the charge {0} are:

it: Sostituendo questi valori otteniamo la formula
en: Substituting these values we obtain the formula

it: La seguente tabella mostra alcune misure di esempio.
en: The following table shows some sample measurements.

it: La cosa importante da osservare in questi risultati
en: The important thing to notice in these results is that the values of the charge {0} are not random. The value of about {1} C appears repeatedly, and then other values appear that are multiples of {2} C, namely {3} C, {4} C and {5} C. Moreover, there are no values smaller than {6} C.

it: Questi risultati provano che la carica accumulata sulle goccioline
en: These results prove that the charge accumulated on the oil droplets is corpuscular, that is, it is made up of a certain number of elementary particles, each of which carries a constant charge of {0} C.

it: È ragionevole pensare che queste particelle siano elettroni.
en: It is reasonable to think that these particles are electrons. So with this experiment we have determined the charge of the electron.

it: In questa scheda abbiamo preso familiarità con un'importante particella elementare
en: In this card we have become familiar with an important elementary particle, the electron. We have described experiments in which the electron behaves like a material particle of classical mechanics. Through these experiments we have seen how the ratio {0} between charge and mass can be measured and, finally, thanks to Millikan's experiment, we have measured the charge {1} C. We have also described an experiment that allows us to gather the first information about the structure of the atom: we have seen that an atom is a neutral system made of a positive nucleus and a certain number of electrons, and we have observed that a nucleus is much heavier than an electron, so it contains almost all the mass of the atom.
